commit.message:
Distinguish TooManySponsoring from LowReserve in trust flag revoke

When AllowTrust/SetTrustLineFlags revokes authorization and redeems
pool-share trustlines into claimable balances, sponsorship sandwich
creation could fail because num_sponsoring is at u32::MAX. Previously
this was unconditionally mapped to LowReserve; now it correctly
propagates as OpTooManySponsoring, matching stellar-core.

Also replaces panic on non-sandwich sponsorship exhaustion with a
proper TooManySponsoring error return.

Fixes #1198

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: henyey
repository.description: A pure Rust implementation of Stellar Core

commit.message:
Fix off-by-one in offer crossing limit

Move maxOffersToCross check to BEFORE cross_offer_v10(), matching
stellar-core which checks offerTrail.size() >= maxOffersToCross before
crossing. Previously the check was after crossing, causing the exact
1000th crossing to be rejected with opEXCEEDED_WORK_LIMIT when
stellar-core allows it.

Fixes #1212

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove broken diagnostics + fix stale Vec pointer in keyed IC

- Remove eprintln diagnostics that called non-existent ba.name() method
- Fix SIGSEGV in keyed load/store IC: derive data pointer from Vec struct
  pointer (stable) instead of cached data_ptr (stale after reallocation)
- The Vec struct lives inside a RefCell and never moves; only its internal
  buffer pointer changes on grow/realloc
- Load IC now reads vec_ptr from IC slot +24, dereferences at offset 0
  to get the current buffer pointer on every fast-path hit

feat(tui): ctrl+s toggles mouse passthrough for terminal text selection

When mouse capture is active (default), terminals route all mouse events
to the TUI, preventing native text selection with drag.

ctrl+s now toggles 'selection mode':
- Disables mouse capture (tea.DisableMouse) so the terminal can handle
  drag-to-select natively
- Shows 'drag to select  •  ctrl+s exit select mode' in the status bar
- Pressing ctrl+s again re-enables mouse capture (tea.EnableMouseCellMotion)

Also:
- Replaces the shift+drag hint in /help with ctrl+s entry (more accurate)
- Adds ctrl+s to coreKeys (extensions cannot override)
- Updates idle status hint to show 'ctrl+s select'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement text sidecar + manifest pipeline for source document processing

- Add core text extraction pipeline for PDFs and images
  - sourceHashing: SHA-256 hashing for files, S3 objects, buffers
  - sourceExtractor: PDF text extraction with Tesseract OCR fallback
  - sidecarWriter: Atomic S3 sidecar writing (temp+rename pattern)
  - manifestService: JSONL manifest operations (load/upsert/save)
  - sourcePipeline: Batch processFolder and verifyFolder orchestration

- Add supporting utilities and tests
  - portablePaths: Cross-platform path handling
  - sourceExtraction.test.ts: Unit tests for hashing, manifest, paths

- Add CLI tool for manual processing and verification
  - scripts/process-sources.ts: process, verify, help commands

- Integrate with server
  - Hook into /api/source-upload endpoint
  - Async processing via setImmediate (non-blocking)

- Add comprehensive documentation
  - SIDECAR_IMPLEMENTATION.md: Complete technical documentation
  - IMPLEMENTATION_SUMMARY.md: High-level overview
  - SIDECAR_QUICK_REFERENCE.md: Quick reference guide

- Add OCR dependency
  - Install tesseract.js (^7.0.0) for image text extraction

Features:
  ✓ Automatic processing on source upload
  ✓ PDF text extraction with OCR fallback
  ✓ Image OCR for .png, .jpg, .jpeg, .tif, .tiff, .webp
  ✓ Atomic sidecar writing to S3
  ✓ JSONL manifest with SHA-256 integrity checks
  ✓ Manifest verification (missing, stale, mismatched detection)
  ✓ Cross-platform path handling
  ✓ Full error handling and recovery
  ✓ Unit test coverage
  ✓ Complete documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix queue eviction ghost account state

Call drop_transaction() at all 3 eviction points in try_add() that
remove from by_hash without cleaning up account_states:
- Lane evictions (pending_eviction_list)
- Expired tx cleanup
- Fee-rate eviction

Previously, evicted transactions left stale account_states entries with
state.transaction = Some(...), causing subsequent submissions from the
same account to be rejected with TryAgainLater.

Matches stellar-core's ban({txToEvict}) → dropTransaction() cleanup.

Fixes #1201

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: preserve leading zero bytes and handle all-0xFF overflow in rich-indexer prefix search upper bound (#5166)

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #5165

Problem Summary:

`get_binary_upper_boundary()` uses `BigUint::to_bytes_be()` which strips
leading zero bytes. For a 22-byte input like `00 01 4c bc ... 86 6e`,
the computed upper bound becomes `01 4c bc ... 86 6f` (21 bytes). Since
bytea comparison is lexicographic, the shorter result is far larger than
intended, causing prefix queries (`args >= $prefix AND args < $upper`)
to massively over-match — e.g. returning 103k scripts instead of 2.

~2.4% of mainnet scripts are affected (args starting with `0x00`),
spanning ACP locks, JoyID, CoTA, and others.

Additionally, when the input is all `0xFF` bytes, incrementing via
`BigUint` produces `[0x01, 0x00, ...]` which is lexicographically
*smaller* than `[0xFF, ...]` in bytea comparison (because `0x01 < 0xFF`
at byte 0), causing the prefix query to match **nothing**.

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:

- Replaced `BigUint` with a direct lexicographic successor computation
on the byte slice using `rposition`: find the rightmost byte that is not
`u8::MAX`, increment it, then truncate everything after it. This
produces the shortest byte string that is strictly greater than every
possible extension of the input prefix, which is exactly what the range
query needs:

```rust
if let Some(i) = value.iter().rposition(|&b| b != u8::MAX) {
    let mut result = value[..=i].to_vec();
    result[i] += 1;
    result
} else {
    // All bytes are u8::MAX — return a sentinel one byte longer
    vec![u8::MAX; value.len() + 1]
}
```

- For the all-`0xFF` overflow case, returns `vec![u8::MAX; value.len() +
1]` — a sentinel that is lexicographically greater than any extension of
the input in bytea comparison.

- Removed the now-unused `num-bigint` dependency from both
`ckb-rich-indexer` (`util/rich-indexer/Cargo.toml`) and the workspace
root (`./Cargo.toml`).

- Uses `u8::MAX` instead of `0xFF` literals throughout for clarity.

- Added 8 regression tests: real-world 22-byte args from the report,
multiple leading zeros, single zero byte, all-`0xFF` overflow sentinel,
carry propagation with truncation (e.g. `[0x00, u8::MAX, u8::MAX]` →
`[0x01]`), short prefix `0x003d`, and standard increment cases.

### Related changes

- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List

Tests

- Unit test

Side effects

- None

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: quake <8990+quake@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: eval-exec <46400566+eval-exec@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: fix invocation count cap that permanently locked out Maglev

increment_invocation_count() previously stopped incrementing at
TURBOFAN_TIERING_THRESHOLD (50). The Maglev deopt cooldown uses
invocation_count to compute next_try_at with exponential backoff.
After just 6 deopts, next_try_at = inv + 2^5 = 82, but the count
was capped at 50 — permanently preventing Maglev re-entry.

Now uses saturating_add(1) so the counter keeps growing. Tiering
checks use == (not >=) so compilation triggers are unaffected.

Also adds diagnostic eprintln for unhandled Maglev IR nodes and
per-deopt logging to aid CI debugging.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Exempt fetch/control messages from global rate limiter

Fetch responses (TxSet, GeneralizedTxSet, ScpQuorumset, DontHave) and
request messages (GetTxSet, GetScpState, GetScpQuorumset) now bypass
the global flood gate rate limiter alongside SCP messages. Previously,
a single peer's flood traffic could exhaust the shared rate bucket and
starve consensus-critical control messages from other peers.

The per-peer rate limiter (PeerRateLimiter) still enforces per-peer
limits on all non-SCP traffic including fetch messages.

Fixes #1214

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent instrument trigger on AdLib file drop

- Add skipPreload option to loadInstruments() to prevent creating
  standalone OPL3Synth WASM instances during AdLib import
- Pass skipPreload: true from both AdLib import paths (primary + companion retry)
- Zero AdPlug streaming player gain on load when autoPlay=false
  (defense-in-depth: ensures no audio leak during initialization)
- Root cause: loadInstruments always called preloadInstruments which
  created an OPL3Synth connected to the audio graph, causing a
  transient sound on file drop

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix invalid SCP envelopes creating immortal pending qset state

Move quorum-set request from before receive_scp_envelope to after it
returns Valid or Pending. Previously, forged envelopes with invalid
signatures would register pending quorum-set entries that persisted
forever, enabling memory exhaustion attacks.

Also add defense-in-depth caps:
- MAX_PENDING_QSET_REQUESTS (512) limits total pending entries
- MAX_PENDING_NODE_IDS (64) limits node IDs per pending entry

Matches stellar-core which verifies envelope signatures before any
quorum-set fetch logic (HerderImpl.cpp:876-893).

Fixes #1205

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix seen-set hashes never clearing on eviction

Clear transaction hashes from the seen set whenever they are removed from
by_hash. Previously, the seen set grew monotonically and evicted/banned/
applied transactions remained marked as seen forever, causing legitimate
re-submissions to be rejected as duplicates.

All 8 by_hash.remove() call sites now also remove from seen:
- Lane evictions during try_add
- Expired tx cleanup during try_add
- Fee-rate eviction during try_add
- Fee-bump replacement during try_add
- evict_expired() age-based removal
- ban() removal
- remove_applied() post-ledger-close cleanup
- shift() age-based auto-ban

Matches stellar-core's prepareDropTransaction → mKnownTxHashes.erase().

Fixes #1171

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: build workspace dependencies before storybook build (#1620)

* fix(ci): build workspace dependencies before storybook build

The Storybook CI build failed because workspace packages
(@heart-of-crown-randomizer/card, @heart-of-crown-randomizer/constraint)
were not built before running `storybook build`. Their dist/ outputs,
required for import resolution, did not exist in CI.

Add a `build-storybook` task to turbo.json with `dependsOn: ["^build"]`
and use `pnpm turbo run build-storybook` in the workflow so Turborepo
automatically builds dependencies first.

Verify: check that the "Deploy Storybook to GitHub Pages" workflow
passes after merging.

commit.message:
Widget v11: fix ghost bullets with height collapse instead of when guard

Instead of using itemName in the when condition (which breaks rendering
because layout params aren't yet populated on initial render), keep
when: lineCount > itemIndex and collapse empty rows to 0dp height with
opacity 0. This hides ghost bullets without preventing initial render.

commit.message:
feat: 2-column table with wrapRecord split-first + split-rest formula

Column 1: wrapRecord contact into split-first (like counter wraps value)
Column 2: pushBack split-rest with \ to wrapped source
Both formulas evaluate correctly for existing rows.
Event graph shows concurrent fork with 2 peers.

Also fixed formula engine:
- isFormulaNode checks tag evaluator registry
- evaluate callback resolves \ with field-aware path
- split-first/split-rest registered as formula tag aliases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(vip): TX monitor position capture, scoreboard integration, and ref model wiring

Issue 1 - Position packets never captured:
  Monitor now classifies packets via subheader source tag (SPADMIC_SRC_POSITION
  vs TDC axis IDs) instead of relying on an unreachable else branch. Both TDC
  and position packets share the 3'b100 header encoding; the subheader word's
  bits [5:4] are used to distinguish them.

Issue 2 - Scoreboard gets no monitor data:
  dispatch_packet() now constructs a spadmic_mon_pkt_txn (new txn subclass
  carrying words, source_id, word_count, is_tdc) and puts it into sb_mb.
  A dedicated mon_to_sb_mb mailbox is created in the environment and wired
  from the monitor to the scoreboard.

Issue 3 - Timestamp truncation:
  captured_packet_t.timestamp changed from int unsigned to longint unsigned
  to hold the full 64-bit $time value.

Issue 4 - Ref models not wired into scoreboard:
  spadmic_tdc_ref_model and spadmic_pos_ref_model are now instantiated as
  members of spadmic_scoreboard. A new monitor_run() task reads from mon_mb,
  validates packets through the ref models, and calls the existing notify_*
  functions. run() forks both stim_run() and monitor_run().

Issue 5 - TDC ref model portability:
  Moved int unsigned expected_wc declaration to top of begin block (before
  executable statements). Fixed OUT_RAW_* enum names to OUT_MODE_* to match
  mptdc_pkg definitions (also fixed in env_cfg and test files).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: remove dead cloud API code (login/logout/whoami/account/deployments/workflows/cloudBuild/cloudExport)

kdeps-io is now a file registry only. All cloud account management, plan gating,
and remote build commands are removed. Registry commands (search/install/publish)
are kept as they remain the interface to kdeps-io.
